mission.title: distribution: Implement asynchronous cleanup of unreferenced blob data
mission.description: Implement asynchronous cleanup of unreferenced blob data. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current distribution deployment
steps.description:
Verify your distribution version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n distribution -l app.kubernetes.io/name=distribution
helm list -n distribution 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working distribution installation.

steps.title: Review distribution configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant distribution configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n distribution -l app.kubernetes.io/name=distribution
kubectl get configmap -n distribution -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=distribution
```
A process should lock and sweep registry data to garbage collect unreferenced. https://github.com/docker/distribution/issues/422#issuecomment-97165192 covers many of the issues will implementing such a solution.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Implement asynchronous cleanup of unreferenced blob data
steps.description:
Pruner tools removes orphaned blobs, dangling references and empty directories.

```
$ pruner -h
pruner allows to print and/or delete registry's orphaned blobs.

Make sure that registry instance isn't running or is running in a read-only
mode before launching this executable.

Usage:
  <config>
```yaml
$ pruner -h
pruner allows to print and/or delete registry's orphaned blobs.

Make sure that registry instance isn't running or is running in a read-only
mode before launching this executable.

Usage:
  <config> [flags]
Flags:
  -c, --confirm=true: ask for confirmation before making changes
  -n, --dry-run=false: perform a trial prune with no changes to registry
  -h, --help=false: help for <config>
  -e, --remove-empty=false: remove empty repositories (containing 0 manifest revisions)
  -v, --verbose=false: turn on verbosive output
  -V, --version=false: show the version and exit
```

steps.title: Upgrade distribution to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade distribution:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade distribution distribution/distribution --namespace distribution
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n distribution
helm list -n distribution
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n distribution -l app.kubernetes.io/name=distribution
kubectl get events -n distribution --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Implement asynchronous cleanup of unreferenced blob data" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Pruner tools removes orphaned blobs, dangling references and empty directories.

```
$ pruner -h
pruner allows to print and/or delete registry's orphaned blobs.

Make sure that registry instance isn't running or is running in a read-only
mode before launching this executable.
codeSnippets:
$ pruner -h
pruner allows to print and/or delete registry's orphaned blobs.

Make sure that registry instance isn't running or is running in a read-only
mode before launching this executable.

Usage:
  <config> [flags]
Flags:
  -c, --confirm=true: ask for confirmation before making changes
  -n, --dry-run=false: perform a trial prune with no changes to registry
  -h, --help=false: help for <config>
  -e, --remove-empty=false: remove empty repositories (containing 0 manifest revisions)
  -v, --verbose=false: turn on verbosive output
  -V, --version=false: show the version and exit

tx := begin()
tx.add(blob)
references := tx.add(manifest)
increment(references)
commit(tx) // must apply all changes or none

tx := begin()
references := tx.delete(manifest)
decrement(references)
commit(tx)

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with distribution installed or the issue environment reproducible.
